a_image.alt: Dreamcore style mood board — Aesthetic · Dreamy & Surreal

sections.a_description: Soft lavender gradients, cloud-like surfaces, and the hazy surrealism of half-remembered dreams made digital — Dreamcore transforms browsing into floating through a pastel-tinted subconscious. The translucent panels, generous border-radius, and muted lilac accents create spaces that feel simultaneously nostalgic and impossible, like childhood memories viewed through frosted glass.

This ethereal style powers ambient music project pages, dream journaling apps, ASMR content platforms, and aesthetic mood board communities. You'll see it on indie game promotional sites with surreal atmospheres, lo-fi music streaming interfaces, sleep and wellness apps, and digital art galleries exploring the boundary between waking perception and dream logic.

The soft, surreal aesthetic of dreamcore — pastel gradients, floating elements, and the hazy quality of half-remembered dreams turned into digital spaces. Background: soft lavender (#f0e6f6) with a dreamlike warmth. The .inner panel has a translucent white background (rgba(255,255,255,0.7)) with a soft shadow (0 8px 32px rgba(180,140,200,0.15)) and 20px border-radius — a cloud-like surface floating in pastel space. Colors: soft lilac (#b490c0) as the primary accent — the color of twilight, lavender fields, and dream sequences. Deep purple-gray (#3d2e4a) for headings, muted lavender (#8a7a92) for body text. The aesthetic is floaty and ethereal — everything feels slightly out of focus, slightly too soft, like browsing through cotton candy. Set in Quicksand — a rounded geometric sans-serif with soft terminals that enhance the dreamy quality. Heading at weight 600, 26px, line-height 1.3, color #3d2e4a. Eyebrow at weight 500, 11px, uppercase, 2px letter-spacing, color #b490c0. Body at weight 400, 15px, line-height 1.7, color #8a7a92. Buttons: primary — lilac (#b490c0), white text, 50px border-radius (pill). Secondary: transparent, lilac border. Alternatives: Nunito (rounded friendly), Comfortaa (rounded display).
{What this palette represents — the core identity}
